body:
Adds simulations wrappers and implements them in the sea breeze experiment.

The Simulation abstraction standardizes how models are run. This is important for two reasons:
1. Standalone models all are built and run in the same way, regardless of component (Atmos, Land, Ocean, etc...) or backend (ClimaCore, Oceananigans). This greatly boosts usability and accessibility.
2. The coupler expects standardized behavior from simulations. This is necessary when writing coupled timesteppers that are readable (using `step!`, and for writing coupling methods (`put, get, push, pull`, etc.)
title: Add basic simulations wrappers
